name: Girosol
description_short: Girosol is an international money transfer service with 30+ years of experience, enabling users to send remittances to 13 countries via agent locations or mobile app.
description_long:
Girosol is an established money transfer company that has been operating for over 30 years, serving immigrant communities in the United States who need to send money to family members abroad. The company operates as a remittance service rather than a traditional check-cashing retailer, though it functions within the broader money services ecosystem. Girosol's primary business model centers on international money transfers to Latin American and Caribbean destinations including Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Haiti, Honduras, and Mexico.

The company offers two primary channels for sending money: through a network of physical agent locations across the United States (accessible via phone at 1-800-GIROSOL) and through a mobile application that allows customers to initiate transfers from their smartphones. Customers can fund transfers using debit cards or bank accounts, and recipients can receive funds through authorized agent locations. The mobile app streamlines the process by allowing users to register once, enter recipient information, specify transfer amounts, and manage all transactions from their device.

Girosol distinguishes itself through competitive pricing focused on affordability, emphasizing that customers "save with every transfer" through favorable exchange rates. The company's 30-year history serves as a trust marker for an immigrant demographic familiar with remittance services. Their dual-channel approach (physical agents plus mobile app) accommodates both technology-comfortable users and those preferring traditional in-person transactions.

However, the website provides minimal specific information about fees, exchange rates, transfer speeds (though "instant" is mentioned), or regulatory details. As a money transfer service rather than a full-service financial institution, Girosol lacks the deposit and lending products of traditional banks, and the site does not detail security certifications, licensing status, or customer protections beyond general trademark language.
address: 2814 N High School Rd, Indianapolis, IN 46224

company_info.headquarters: 2814 N High School Rd, Indianapolis, IN 46224

pros: 30+ years of operating history and experience in remittance services
Serves 13 countries across Latin America and the Caribbean
Mobile app allows registration once with unlimited future transfers
Dual-channel access: physical agent network plus smartphone app
Competitive rates marketed as allowing customers to save on transfers
Debit card and bank account funding options for flexibility
Large network of independent and certified payment locations across U.S. states
Instant transfer capability mentioned on homepage
cons: Website does not disclose specific transfer fees or exchange rate markups
No information about transfer delivery timeframes beyond 'instant' claims
Limited details on security features, encryption, or fraud protection
No transparent disclosure of regulatory licensing or compliance certifications
Customer service primarily phone-based (1-800 number) with no live chat or email support visible
best_for: Immigrants sending remittances to family in Latin America and the Caribbean
Unbanked or underbanked individuals preferring agent-based money transfer
Mobile-first users comfortable managing transfers via smartphone app
Repeat senders seeking convenient, affordable international money transfers
diagnosis: Girosol is best for immigrants and frequent senders needing affordable remittances to Latin America and the Caribbean, particularly those comfortable using mobile apps or preferring agent locations. The main caveat is the website's lack of transparency regarding fees, exact transfer speeds, and regulatory credentials—essential information for comparing this service against competitors.
services: Mobile transaction management and transfer tracking
